body_txt: In cases where a character is so good-looking, kind, muscular, badass, etc. they may suffer the misfortune (or not ) of being found attractive by members of their own sex, and only by their own sex. Note: A character can only qualify for this if people of the opposite sex show up in the series and clearly show no romantic or sexual interest in them. So if there are only members of the same sex that show up , they don't count. Could develop into Chick Magnet or Dude Magnet later on if members of the opposite gender do eventually show interest. If the male character is a Bishōnen it could be a case of So Beautiful, It's a Curse or of only men wanting to go for him because he looks like a beautiful girl and the men may justify still being straight by pointing this out . For females, the most common reason they may not attract any guys is androgyny, which can make it difficult for guys to tell whether the girl in question is a feminine looking male , or a boyish looking female. Naturally, this ambiguity tends to "scare off" potential male suitors for fear of guessing wrong . Other reasons range from being seen as unattractive , or too tomboyish, or rude , or too temperamental to even approach. If the character is a big enough same-sex magnet this could be another reason they don't attract people of the opposite sex because the opposite-sex characters are jealous of the magnetic pull the character has on their crushes. (For example, a guy may attract numerous guys some of who are chick magnets and their female admirers see the pheromone releasing male to be in the way of them being with their crushes to the point of jealousy and this can go both ways if a female character attracts all the female characters in the vicinity that the guys are crushing on.) Stupid Sexy Flanders and I Didn't Mean to Turn You On are also potential outcomes of only attracting suitors from one's own gender, as their would-be hopefuls may decide that they don't care even after finding out. Bara, Yaoi or Yuri genre can still appear here when other gender characters appear in those shows. Compare Unwanted Harem and Abhorrent Admirer . Compare/contrast Even the Guys Want Him and Even the Girls Want Her .
